steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student wants to place the 1821 Declaration of Independence in the context of Valle's changing political beliefs. The correct choice must connect three elements from the notes: (1) Valle had long opposed independence, (2) Riego's 1820 revolt changed his mind, and (3) Valle wrote the 1821 Declaration. All three elements must be present and accurate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Colonel Riego's revolt was the inspiration that Valle, a long-standing opponent of Central American independence, needed to change his political beliefs': Mentions the revolt and Valle's prior opposition, but does not connect the changed beliefs to the writing of the 1821 Declaration. The goal is to place the Declaration in context, which this choice fails to do. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Long an opponent of Central American independence, Valle changed his mind after an 1820 revolt and wrote the 1821 declaration': Captures all three required elements: Valle's prior opposition, the 1820 revolt as the turning point, and his writing of the 1821 Declaration. This places the Declaration directly in the context of his changing beliefs. ✓
  • Choice C — 'A change in Valle's political beliefs that occurred when the Spanish king repealed the 1812 constitution led to Valle writing Central America's Declaration of Independence': Gets the cause wrong — according to the notes, Valle changed his mind after Riego's 1820 revolt, not when the king repealed the 1812 constitution (which happened in 1814, years earlier). ✗
  • Choice D — 'The writing of Central America's Declaration of Independence may not have happened were it not for Colonel Riego's 1820 revolt': Uses speculative language ("may not have happened") and omits Valle's changing political beliefs entirely, which are central to the stated goal. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
